question_text: A compressed spring is released and shoots a ball across a table. Calculate the kinetic energy of the ball if its mass is 0.2 kg and it travels at 10 m/s. (KE = ½ × m × v²)
explanation: Kinetic energy is calculated using KE = ½ × m × v². Substituting: KE = ½ × 0.2 × 10² = ½ × 0.2 × 100 = 10 J. This energy came from the elastic potential energy stored in the compressed spring.
